title: Nematanthus monanthos
taxonomy: Nematanthus monanthos is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the African violet family. This family includes over 3,000 species of plants, primarily native to tropical and subtropical regions. Nematanthus monanthos is a type of epiphyte, often grown for its ornamental value and ability to thrive in a variety of conditions. As a member of the Gesneriaceae family, Nematanthus monanthos shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as its small, delicate flowers and waxy leaves. The classification of Nematanthus monanthos as a Gesneriad is also reflected in its growth habits and requirements, which are typical of plants in this family.
uses: Nematanthus monanthos has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. In some cultures, Nematanthus monanthos is used to treat skin conditions such as acne and wounds due to its purported antibacterial and antifungal properties. However, further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal efficacy and safety. More studies are required to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Nematanthus monanthos for medicinal purposes.

properties: Nematanthus monanthos, also known as the Goldfish Plant, has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential therapeutic properties.
